title: cuphea scaberrima
intro: Cuphea scaberrima, also known as the hairy cuphea or hairy cupheal, is a plant species within the Lythraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including its antimicrobial properties which can be used to treat infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Cuphea scaberrima are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic activities. Herbal preparations of Cuphea scaberrima are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.
